supersedes: New dated folder for 2026-07-17 (two days before the final). The full Run 3+4+5 text is preserved in research/2026-07-15/findings.md and Run 6 in research/2026-07-16/findings.md, all in git; this folder is the Run 7 pass. No match has been played since Run 5, so the bracket is unchanged.

ESP.optaNote: CHANGED from Run 6: Opta NOW prints an explicit Spain-v-Argentina final simulation (25,000 sims, published Jul 16): Spain ~56.05-56.31% v Argentina ~43.69-43.95% across syndication (representative .561/.439 adopted). This RETIRES the derived .569/.431 and drops the conditioning language. The printed pair sits a shade closer to Argentina than the old derivation (43.9% vs 43.1%) because Opta is now simulating the actual final rather than back-solving a pre-SF2 trophy column. Still well inside the author range.

market.moveDirection: consolidated at the Run 6 level; the two-day drift toward Spain has settled
market.note: Re-checked Jul 17: the market held its Jul 16 level rather than moving further. Kalshi ~58.2c, Polymarket ~58.0c Spain; DraftKings 90-min Spain +115 unchanged. Named trophy moneylines drew no fresh re-quote; overround-normalized trophy market still ~59/41 Spain.

caesars.note: no fresh re-quote since Jul 15; carried

anchorConvergence.note: Four independent anchors inside a five-point spread, all inside the unchanged author range. Opta's now-explicit final sim (~56/44) replaces the prior derivation and keeps the cluster tight.

H1_deliberate_favoritism.note: Gains an againstPoint: the final referee is a European (UEFA) official, confederation-non-neutral toward Spain, not Argentina - the reverse of what favoritism toward Argentina would arrange.

H2_chargeable_conduct.note: Quiet pass; no subpoena, indictment, or formal cooperating witness reported. Loose 'federal grand jury' syndication phrasing appears but the changeMind trigger is not met. Still pressing toward the upper half of its range without a formal step.

H4_variance_perception.note: Gains a forPoint: the final-referee appointment (Vincic, a European for a Spain match) drew instant social-media grumbling despite being a form-based, CL-final-calibre pick - perception attaching to a neutral appointment. Band held; H4's outstanding test is the final itself.

outcomes.note: No match played since Run 5. Final: Spain v Argentina, Jul 19, MetLife, 3 p.m. ET. Referee now known (Vincic). Grading of Run 1-6 scorecards belongs to the score step.
outcomes.finalReferee: Slavko Vincic (Slovenia/UEFA); assistants Klancnik & Kovacic (SVN); 4th official Makhadmeh (JOR); VAR Dankert (GER); AVAR Gallo (COL); support VAR Al Marri (QAT)

carriedForwardFromRun6: All Run 1-6 verified facts stand: Spain 2-0 France with the disputed Digne-on-Yamal penalty; England 1-2 Argentina (Gordon 55'; E. Fernandez 85', L. Martinez 90+2' from a Messi cross; ARG 64% possession, xG ~1.84 v ~0.53; referee Elfath, no controversy of substance); market flip to Spain; five named banks; Miami Herald ~$90M; La Nacion $260M/$57M attribution; Tofoni ~3h videoconference (witness, not charged); the Jul 14 'from inside' inward turn (former AFA insiders, informal videoconferences, three named prosecutors across DC/SDFL, 'cuevas' mechanism, Toviggino>Tapia perceived focus); domestic 'mansion' case hearing set Aug 12; the multi-million-signature expulsion petition and Collina/Scaloni rebuttals; Tapia travel-authorization nugget; no US charges; FBI declines comment; no suspensions for the final; author ranges ESP 55-60 / ARG 40-45.
newSinceRun6: FINAL REFEREE APPOINTED (biggest item): FIFA named Slavko Vincic (Slovenia/UEFA) for the Jul 19 final, announced Jul 17. First Slovenian, 23rd person to referee a men's WC final. Crew: assistants Klancnik & Kovacic (SVN), 4th official Makhadmeh (JOR), reserve AR Al-Kalaf (JOR), VAR Dankert (GER), AVAR Gallo (COL), support VAR Al Marri (QAT). This CONTRADICTS Run 6's 'neutral field' reasoning: the operative FIFA pattern is European refs for finals OUTSIDE Europe, so a UEFA official was always plausible; tipped frontrunner Faghani (Iran) did NOT get it. Editorial read: a European ref for a Spain-Argentina final is confederation-non-neutral toward SPAIN, not Argentina - cuts AGAINST H1, and is fresh H4 perception fuel (instant social-media grumbling) despite being a form-based, CL-final-calibre pick. Context: lands amid FIFA-UEFA friction (Infantino-Ceferin, suspended Balogun ban). Vincic history: only prior Argentina match was their 2022 shock loss to Saudi Arabia; refereed Spain several times unbeaten; Real Madrid criticised an April CL red (Bellingham 'a joke'), Mourinho praised him. Added timeline node final-referee (order 15), sources fifa-final-referee + espn-vincic, H1 againstPoint + H4 forPoint. H1 and H4 bands HELD.

OPTA NOW PRINTS AN EXPLICIT FINAL PAIR (closes a Run 6 open item): direct Spain-v-Argentina simulation (25,000 sims, Jul 16): Spain ~56.05-56.31% v Argentina ~43.69-43.95% (representative .561/.439). Retires the derived .569/.431 and drops the conditioning language. Slightly closer to Argentina than the derivation (43.9 vs 43.1) - Opta simulating the actual final, not back-solving a trophy column. Still inside the author range. models.ts OPTA updated (asOf Jul 16, teams .561/.439, comment rewritten, publishedPostSF1 kept); whowins-intro prose updated both locales.

MARKET CONSOLIDATED, not moved: Kalshi ~58.2, Polymarket ~58.0 Spain (flat vs Run 6's ~58.5/58.4); DraftKings 90-min Spain +115 unchanged. Named trophy lines carried; ~59/41 normalized. BOOKMAKERS.asOf -> 2026-07-17. AUTHOR_RANGES unchanged.

MONEY THREAD QUIET: no verified movement since the Jul 14 inward turn. Syndication of the established corpus; some loose 'federal grand jury' phrasing but no subpoena/indictment/formal cooperating witness reported. H2 changeMind trigger NOT met; bands HELD. No new financial timeline node this pass.

SQUAD reconfirmed two days out: Spain's Yamal (thigh strapping) & Porro (muscle overload) trained apart again but expected fit, expected to rejoin full training from Friday; neither serious. Argentina clean; Messi leads Golden Boot on 8. Predicted XIs are previews, not adopted.

SILVER still paywalled: final preview exists (Spain 13-1 goal diff, long unbeaten run noted; Argentina's late England comeback praised) but per-team title split behind the paywall. Methodology + market reference retained.
